channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: To stop leaks, the Trump administration wants federal workers to sign NDAs
item.description: The Trump administration has proposed creating a new government-wide nondisclosure agreement for new and existing federal employees.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has proposed creating a new government-wide nondisclosure agreement for new and existing federal employees.

(Image credit: Michael A. McCoy)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Gulf shrimpers want help from Congress as fuel costs climb
item.description: The declining number of Gulf shrimpers who are still in business are now struggling as gas prices rise and competition with cheaper imports remains high.
item.content:encoded:
The declining number of Gulf shrimpers who are still in business are now struggling as gas prices rise and competition with cheaper imports remains high.

(Image credit: Jay Marcano for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Drew Hawkins

item.title: After Stephen Colbert's viral talk show parody, CBS backs down from copyright action
item.description: CBS and Paramount backed away from copyright challenges to limit distribution of Stephen Colbert's appearance on a Michigan cable access show. He ended his run as host of "The Late Show" on Thursday.
item.content:encoded:
CBS and Paramount backed away from copyright challenges to limit distribution of Stephen Colbert's appearance on a Michigan cable access show. He ended his run as host of "The Late Show" on Thursday.

(Image credit: Evan Agostini)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Diners are staying home, so this restaurant lets patrons pay what they want
item.description: Americans are increasingly passing up on dining out. So one restaurant is allowing diners to pay what they like for their food.
item.content:encoded:
Americans are increasingly passing up on dining out. So one restaurant is allowing diners to pay what they like for their food.

(Image credit: Sergio Flores for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Torn by war, Israelis and Palestinians tie their fortunes together
item.description: At a time when hopes are dim for resolving the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, some Arab and Jewish entrepreneurs are partnering across the divide, hoping to prove what's possible.
item.content:encoded:
At a time when hopes are dim for resolving the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, some Arab and Jewish entrepreneurs are partnering across the divide, hoping to prove what's possible.

(Image credit: Dena Yadin)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Summer electric bills sizzle as the cost of cooling climbs
item.description: The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: One solution for Maine's struggling fishing industry? Give fillets away for free
item.description: Surging food costs and fuel prices are pummeling Maine's struggling groundfishing industry. But a pandemic-era program is helping to keep it afloat as inflation worsens.
item.content:encoded:
Surging food costs and fuel prices are pummeling Maine's struggling groundfishing industry. But a pandemic-era program is helping to keep it afloat as inflation worsens.

(Image credit: Ryan David Brown for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Ask AI or just Google it? Google makes a big change to a little search box
item.description: The search giant is updating its famously minimalist homepage. But what looks like a tiny design change is a very big deal.
item.content:encoded:
The search giant is updating its famously minimalist homepage. But what looks like a tiny design change is a very big deal.

(Image credit: GLENN CHAPMAN/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Shein buys Everlane, which sold millennials the dream of ethical, affordable luxury
item.description: Everlane's finances have faltered in recent years. But will the merger alienate Everlane's existing shoppers — or sway droves of Shein fans to trade up?
item.content:encoded:
Everlane's finances have faltered in recent years. But will the merger alienate Everlane's existing shoppers — or sway droves of Shein fans to trade up?

(Image credit: Smith Collection/Gado/Getty Images
)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Drive slower, go electric, don't drive at all? The best options for saving gas
item.description: The national average for a gallon of gasoline is $4.55, as America heads into one of the busiest travel weekends of the year. What can you do to cut your costs?
item.content:encoded:
The national average for a gallon of gasoline is $4.55, as America heads into one of the busiest travel weekends of the year. What can you do to cut your costs?

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske
